segments.text: After these things, when Artaxerxes the king of the Persians reigned, Esdras came, who was the son of Azaraias, the son of Zechrias, the son of Helkias, the son of Salem,

the son of Sadduk, the son of Ahitob, the son of Amarias, the son of Ozias,  the son of Memeroth, the son of Zaraias, the son of Savias, the son of Boccas, the son of Abisne, the son of Phinees, the son of Eleazar, the son of Aaron, the chief priest.
This Esdras went up from Babylon as a skilled scribe in the law of Moses, which was given by the God of Israel.
The king honored him, for he found favor in his sight in all his requests.
There went up with him also some of the children of Israel, and of the priests, Levites, holy singers, gatekeepers, and temple servants to Jerusalem

in the seventh year of the reign of Artaxerxes, in the fifth month (this was the king’s seventh year); for they left Babylon on the new moon of the first month and came to Jerusalem, by the prosperous journey which the Lord gave them for his sake.
For Esdras had very great skill, so that he omitted nothing of the law and commandments of the Lord, but taught all Israel the ordinances and judgments.
Now the commission, which was written from King Artaxerxes, came to Esdras the priest and reader of the law of the Lord, was as follows:
King Artaxerxes to Esdras the priest and reader of the law of the Lord, greetings.
segments.speaker: King Artaxerxes

segments.text: Having determined to deal graciously, I have given orders that those of the nation of the Jews, and of the priests and Levites, and of those within our realm who are willing and freely choose to, should go with you to Jerusalem.
segments.speaker: King Artaxerxes

segments.text: As many therefore as are so disposed, let them depart with you, as it has seemed good both to me and my seven friends the counselors,
segments.speaker: King Artaxerxes

segments.text: that they may look to the affairs of Judea and Jerusalem, in accordance with what is in the Lord’s law,
segments.speaker: King Artaxerxes

segments.text: and carry the gifts to the Lord of Israel to Jerusalem, which I and my friends have vowed, and that all the gold and silver that can be found in the country of Babylonia for the Lord in Jerusalem,
segments.speaker: King Artaxerxes

segments.text: with that also which is given of the people for the temple of the Lord their God that is at Jerusalem, be collected: even the gold and silver for bulls, rams, and lambs, and what goes with them,
segments.speaker: King Artaxerxes

segments.text: to the end that they may offer sacrifices to the Lord upon the altar of the Lord their God, which is in Jerusalem.
segments.speaker: King Artaxerxes

segments.text: Whatever you and your kindred decide to do with gold and silver, do that according to the will of your God.
segments.speaker: King Artaxerxes

segments.text: The holy vessels of the Lord, which are given you for the use of the temple of your God, which is in Jerusalem,
segments.speaker: King Artaxerxes

segments.text: and whatever else you shall remember for the use of the temple of your God, you shall give it out of the king’s treasury.
segments.speaker: King Artaxerxes

segments.text: I, King Artaxerxes, have also commanded the keepers of the treasures in Syria and Phoenicia, that whatever Esdras the priest and reader of the law of the Most High God shall send for, they should give it to him with all diligence,
segments.speaker: King Artaxerxes

segments.text: to the sum of one hundred talents of silver, likewise also of wheat even to one hundred cors, and one hundred firkins of wine, and salt in abundance.
segments.speaker: King Artaxerxes

segments.text: Let all things be performed after God’s law diligently to the most high God, that wrath come not upon the kingdom of the king and his sons.
segments.speaker: King Artaxerxes

segments.text: I command you also that no tax, nor any other imposition, be laid upon any of the priests, or Levites, or holy singers, or gatekeepers, or temple servants, or any that have employment in this temple, and that no man has authority to impose any tax on them.
segments.speaker: King Artaxerxes

segments.text: You, Esdras, according to the wisdom of God, ordain judges and justices that they may judge in all Syria and Phoenicia all those who know the law of your God; and those who don’t know it, you shall teach.
segments.speaker: King Artaxerxes

segments.text: Whoever transgresses the law of your God and of the king shall be punished diligently, whether it be by death, or other punishment, by penalty of money, or by imprisonment.
segments.speaker: King Artaxerxes

segments.text: Then Esdras the scribe said, {
Blessed be the only Lord, the God of my fathers, who has put these things into the heart of the king, to glorify his house that is in Jerusalem,
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: and has honored me in the sight of the king, his counselors, and all his friends and nobles.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: Therefore I was encouraged by the help of the Lord my God, and gathered together out of Israel men to go up with me.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: These are the chief according to their families and their several divisions, who went up with me from Babylon in the reign of King Artaxerxes:
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: of the sons of Phinees, Gerson; of the sons of Ithamar, Gamael; of the sons of David,  Attus the son of Sechenias;
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: of the sons of Phoros, Zacharais; and with him were counted one hundred fifty men;
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: of the sons of Phaath Moab, Eliaonias the son of Zaraias, and with him two hundred men;
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: of the sons of Zathoes, Sechenias the son of Jezelus, and with him three hundred men; of the sons of Adin, Obeth the son of Jonathan, and with him two hundred fifty men;
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: of the sons of Elam, Jesias son of Gotholias, and with him seventy men;
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: of the sons of Saphatias, Zaraias son of Michael, and with him seventy men;
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: of the sons of Joab, Abadias son of Jehiel. Jezelus, and with him two hundred twelve men;
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: of the sons of Banias, Salimoth son of Josaphias, and with him one hundred sixty men;
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: of the sons of Babi, Zacharias son of Bebai, and with him twenty-eight men;
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: of the sons of Azgad: Astath, Joannes son of Hakkatan Akatan, and with him one hundred ten men;
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: of the sons of Adonikam, the last, and these are the names of them, Eliphalat, Jeuel, and Samaias, and with them seventy men;
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: of the sons of Bago, Uthi the son of Istalcurus, and with him seventy men.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: I gathered them together to the river called Theras. There we pitched our tents three days, and I inspected them.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: When I had found there none of the priests and Levites,
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: then sent I to Eleazar, Iduel, Maasmas,
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: Elnathan, Samaias, Joribus, Nathan, Ennatan, Zacharias, and Mosollamus, principal men and men of understanding.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: I asked them to go to Loddeus the captain, who was in the place of the treasury,
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: and commanded them that they should speak to Loddeus, to his kindred, and to the treasurers in that place, to send us such men as might execute the priests’ office in our Lord’s house.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: By the mighty hand of our Lord, they brought to us men of understanding of the sons of Mooli the son of Levi, the son of Israel,  Asebebias, and his sons, and his kindred, who were eighteen,
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: and Asebias, Annuus, and Osaias his brother, of the sons of Chanuneus, and their sons were twenty men;
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: and of the temple servants whom David and the principal men had appointed for the servants of the Levites, two hundred twenty temple servants. The list of all their names was reported.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: There I vowed a fast for the young men before our Lord, to seek from him a prosperous journey both for us and for our children and livestock that were with us;
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: for I was ashamed to ask of the king infantry, cavalry, and an escort for protection against our adversaries.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: For we had said to the king that the power of our Lord would be with those who seek him, to support them in all ways.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: Again we prayed to our lord about these things, and found him to be merciful.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: Then I set apart twelve men of the chiefs of the priests,  Eserebias, Assamias, and ten men of their kindred with them.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: I weighed out to them the silver, the gold, and the holy vessels of the house of our Lord, which the king, his counselors, the nobles, and all Israel had given.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: When I had weighed it, I delivered to them six hundred fifty talents of silver, silver vessels weighing one hundred talents, one hundred talents of gold,
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: twenty golden vessels, and twelve vessels of brass, even of fine brass, glittering like gold.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: I said to them, “You are holy to the Lord, the vessels are holy, and the gold and the silver are a vow to the Lord, the Lord of our fathers.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: Watch and keep them until you deliver them to the chiefs of the priests and Levites, and to the principal men of the families of Israel in Jerusalem, in the chambers of our Lord’s house.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: So the priests and the Levites who received the silver, the gold, and the vessels which were in Jerusalem, brought them into the temple of the Lord.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: We left the river Theras on the twelfth day of the first month. We came to Jerusalem by the mighty hand of our Lord which was upon us. The Lord delivered us from from every enemy on the way, and so we came to Jerusalem.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: When we had been there three days, the silver and gold was weighed and delivered in our Lord’s house on the fourth day to Marmoth the priest the son of Urias.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: With him was Eleazar the son of Phinees, and with them were Josabdus the son of Jesus and Moeth the son of Sabannus, the Levites. All was delivered to them by number and weight.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: All the weight of them was recorded at the same hour.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: Moreover those who had come out of captivity offered sacrifices to the Lord, the God of Israel, even twelve bulls for all Israel, ninety-six rams,
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: seventy-two lambs, and twelve goats for a peace offering—all of them a sacrifice to the Lord.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: They delivered the king’s commandments to the king’s stewards and to the governors of Coelesyria and Phoenicia; and they honored the people and the temple of the Lord.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: Now when these things were done, the principal men came to me and said,
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: “The nation of Israel, the princes, the priests, and the Levites haven’t put away from themselves the foreign people of the land nor the uncleannesses of the Gentiles—the Canaanites, Hittites, Pherezites, Jebusites, Moabites, Egyptians, and Edomites.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: For both they and their sons have married with their daughters, and the holy seed is mixed with the foreign people of the land. From the beginning of this matter the rulers and the nobles have been partakers of this iniquity.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: And as soon as I had heard these things, I tore my clothes and my holy garment, and plucked the hair from off my head and beard, and sat down sad and full of heaviness.
So all those who were moved at the word of the Lord, the God of Israel, assembled to me while I mourned for the iniquity, but I sat still full of heaviness until the evening sacrifice.
Then rising up from the fast with my clothes and my holy garment torn, and bowing my knees and stretching out my hands to the Lord,
I said, {
O Lord, I am ashamed and confounded before your face,
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: for our sins are multiplied above our heads, and our errors have reached up to heaven
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: ever since the time of our fathers. We are in great sin, even to this day.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: For our sins and our fathers’ we with our kindred, our kings, and our priests were given up to the kings of the earth, to the sword, and to captivity, and for a prey with shame, to this day.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: Now in some measure mercy has been shown to us from you, O Lord, that there should be left us a root and a name in the place of your sanctuary,
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: and to uncover a light in the house of the Lord our God, and to give us food in the time of our servitude.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: Yes, when we were in bondage, we were not forsaken by our Lord, but he gave us favor before the kings of Persia, so that they gave us food,
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: glorified the temple of our Lord, and raised up the desolate Zion, to give us a sure dwelling in Judea and Jerusalem.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: “Now, O Lord, what shall we say, having these things? For we have transgressed your commandments which you gave by the hand of your servants the prophets, saying,
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: ‘The land, which you enter into to possess as an inheritance, is a land polluted with the pollutions of the foreigners of the land, and they have filled it with their uncleanness.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: Therefore now you shall not join your daughters to their sons, neither shall you take their daughters for your sons.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: You shall never seek to have peace with them, that you may be strong, and eat the good things of the land, and that you may leave it for an inheritance to your children for evermore.’
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: All that has happened is done to us for our wicked works and great sins, for you, O Lord, made our sins light,
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: and gave to us such a root; but we have turned back again to transgress your law in mingling ourselves with the uncleanness of the heathen of the land.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: You weren’t angry with us to destroy us until you had left us neither root, seed, nor name.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: O Lord of Israel, you are true, for we are left a root this day.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: Behold, now we are before you in our iniquities, for we can’t st and any longer before you because of these things.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: As Esdras in his prayer made his confession, weeping, and lying flat on the ground before the temple, a very great throng of men, women, and children gathered to him from Jerusalem; for there was great weeping among the multitude.
Then Jechonias the son of Jeelus, one of the sons of Israel, called out, and said, {
O Esdras, we have sinned against the Lord God, we have married foreign women of the heathen of the land, but there is still hope for Israel.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: Let’s make an oath to the Lord about this, that we will put away all our foreign wives with their children,
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: as seems good to you, and to as many as obey the Lord’s Law.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: Arise, and take action, for this is your task, and we will be with you to do valiantly.
segments.speaker: Esdras the scribe

segments.text: So Esdras arose, and took an oath from the chief of the priests and Levites of all Israel to do these things; and they swore to it.
